raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/2025, I purchased a pair of sneakers from XXXX XXXX for {$110.00} using my Macy 's American Express card. 

The sneakers were purchased as a gift and remain unused, unworn, and in their original packaging with tags attached. When I attempted to return the item, the merchant refused a refund and stated that in-store purchases were subject to a no-refund or exchange-only policy. 

This policy was not disclosed to me at the time of purchase, was not printed on the receipt, and was not available on the merchant 's website. After contacting the merchant, I received written correspondence stating that there was " no documentation confirming what was disclosed at the point of sale or on my receipt. '' The merchant also stated that my claim involved potential undisclosed restrictions requiring further review and that I would be contacted by a specialist. However, after those initial responses, the merchant stopped responding to my communications and never provided a resolution. 

I disputed the charge with Macy 's Credit Services and provided the receipt and the merchant 's written statements. The dispute was initially denied as a billing dispute and later denied again because the merchant stated it was enforcing its no-refund policy. 

My concern is that the dispute was evaluated based on the existence of the merchant 's policy rather than whether that policy was properly disclosed before or at the time of purchase. The merchant acknowledged there is no documentation showing what was disclosed at the point of sale or on my receipt. Despite this, Macy 's denied the dispute without addressing the lack of disclosure. 

I am requesting that Macy 's Credit Services conduct a fair review of the dispute and consider whether the merchant adequately disclosed the return restrictions before relying on that policy to deny a refund.
